perturbation.original_text: Sponsor shall during the term of this Agreement -------------------- receive the following sponsorship benefits:
perturbation.changed_text: Sponsor shall during the term of this Agreement -------------------- generally receive the following sponsorship benefits:
perturbation.explanation: The insertion of the word "generally" weakens the obligation to provide all listed sponsorship benefits. It introduces ambiguity as to whether all benefits are guaranteed or if some may be subject to change or omission at FNW's discretion.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Washington Consumer Protection Act prohibits unfair or deceptive acts or practices in trade or commerce. Promising specific sponsorship benefits and then failing to provide them due to the ambiguous 'generally' clause could be construed as a deceptive practice if consumers (in this case, the Sponsor, Mercata, Inc.) are led to believe they will receive all listed benefits but do not. The word "generally" introduces a loophole, potentially allowing FNW to avoid fulfilling all promised benefits without clearly disclosing this possibility upfront, thereby contradicting the CPA's aim to ensure fair dealing.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: RCW 19.86.020: Unfair competition, practices, declared unlawful. Print RCWs > Title 19 > Chapter 19.86 > Section 19.86.020 19.86.010 << 19.86.020 >> 19.86.023 PDF RCW 19.86.020 Unfair competition, practices, declared unlawful. Unfair methods of competition and unfair or deceptive acts or practices in the conduct of any trade or commerce are hereby declared unlawful. [ 1961 c 216 s 2 .] NOTES: Hearing instrument dispensing, advertising, etc. — Application: RCW 18.35.180 .

perturbation.justification: The insertion of "generally" weakens the obligation to provide sponsorship benefits, potentially violating the Washington Consumer Protection Act. RCW 19.86.020 declares unlawful "unfair or deceptive acts or practices in the conduct of any trade or commerce." By promising specific benefits and then potentially not delivering them due to the ambiguity introduced by "generally," the modified text creates a deceptive practice, thus contradicting the law.

perturbation.original_text: The content and design of Sponsor's creative ---------------------------- material shall be within the discretion of Sponsor. However, if FNW objects to any such material when it is provided or thereafter, it shall notify Sponsor as soon as possible and Sponsor shall take prompt action to address FNW's concerns.
perturbation.changed_text: The content and design of Sponsor's creative ---------------------------- material shall be within the discretion of Sponsor. However, if FNW expresses reservations about any such material, Sponsor should consider FNW's concerns.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "FNW objects...Sponsor shall take prompt action to address FNW's concerns" to "FNW expresses reservations...Sponsor should consider FNW's concerns", the obligation for the sponsor to actually address concerns is removed. The Sponsor is only expected to "consider" FNW's reservations, which weakens the level of commitment to addressing FNW's concerns.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Washington Uniform Commercial Code - Good Faith (RCW 62A.1-304)
perturbation.law_explanation: RCW 62A.1-304 states that every contract or duty within the UCC imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement. By only requiring the Sponsor to "consider" concerns without a mandate to address them, the modified language allows the Sponsor to potentially disregard legitimate concerns from FNW, thereby acting in bad faith. This undermines the implicit understanding that both parties will work together to ensure the smooth execution of the agreement.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: RCW 62A.1-304: Obligation of good faith. Print RCWs > Title 62A > Chapter 62A.1 > Section 62A.1-304 62A.1-303 << 62A.1-304 >> 62A.1-305 PDF RCW 62A.1-304 Obligation of good faith. Every contract or duty within this title imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement. [ 2012 c 214 s 118 .] NOTES: Application — Savings — 2012 c 214: See notes following RCW 62A.1-101 .
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the obligation of the Sponsor to address FNW's concerns. The original text states "Sponsor shall take prompt action to address FNW's concerns," creating a clear obligation. The changed text replaces this with "Sponsor should consider FNW's concerns," which removes the mandatory action. RCW 62A.1-304 states that "Every contract or duty within this title imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement." By weakening the Sponsor's obligation to address concerns, the changed text allows the Sponsor to potentially disregard legitimate concerns, thus undermining the obligation of good faith, which the law requires.

perturbation.original_text: FNW agrees to defend, indemnify and hold Sponsor harmless --------------- from and against all claims, suits, liabilities, costs and expenses, including reasonable attorney costs and fees, for injury to, including death of, persons (whether they be third persons or employees of either of the parties hereto) or any loss of or damage to property in any manner arising from or relating to the rights 3. conveyed herein, with the understanding that this obligation shall not apply to, and Sponsor agrees to defend, indemnify and hold FNW and its officers, directors, employees and agents harmless from and against, all losses, claims, suits, demands, actions, liabilities, costs and expenses, including reasonable attorney costs and fees, for injury to, including death of, persons (whether they be third persons or employees of either of the parties hereto) or any loss of or damage to property in any manner arising from the content of any advertising copy supplied by Sponsor or the negligence or intentional misconduct of Sponsor or its officers, employees or agents.
perturbation.changed_text: FNW agrees to, when feasible, defend, indemnify and hold Sponsor harmless --------------- from and against all claims, suits, liabilities, costs and expenses, including reasonable attorney costs and fees, for injury to, including death of, persons (whether they be third persons or employees of either of the parties hereto) or any loss of or damage to property in any manner arising from or relating to the rights 3. conveyed herein, with the understanding that this obligation shall not apply to, and Sponsor agrees to use good faith efforts to defend, indemnify and hold FNW and its officers, directors, employees and agents harmless from and against, all losses, claims, suits, demands, actions, liabilities, costs and expenses, including reasonable attorney costs and fees, for injury to, including death of, persons (whether they be third persons or employees of either of the parties hereto) or any loss of or damage to property in any manner arising from the content of any advertising copy supplied by Sponsor or the negligence or intentional misconduct of Sponsor or its officers, employees or agents.
perturbation.explanation: The modifications inject ambiguity into the indemnification obligations. For FNW, the phrase "when feasible" makes their duty to defend, indemnify, and hold harmless dependent on subjective circumstances, rendering the extent of this obligation unclear. For the Sponsor, the change from a strict agreement to defend and indemnify to "use good faith efforts" to do so weakens their obligation. What constitutes "good faith efforts" is subjective and could potentially allow the Sponsor to avoid full indemnification even in situations where they should be responsible.
perturbation.law_explanation: RCW 19.36.010 requires certain agreements to be in writing and signed to be enforceable. While the agreement is in writing, making the obligation to indemnify "when feasible" and to "use good faith efforts", significantly undermines the specificity and clarity required for enforceable contracts, and introduces a level of uncertainty that could render the obligation unenforceable.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: RCW 19.36.010: Contracts, etc., void unless in writing. Print RCWs > Title 19 > Chapter 19.36 > Section 19.36.010 Beginning of Chapter << 19.36.010 >> 19.36.020 PDF RCW 19.36.010 Contracts, etc., void unless in writing. In the following cases, specified in this section, any agreement, contract, and promise shall be void, unless such agreement, contract, or promise, or some note or memorandum thereof, be in writing, and signed by the party to be charged therewith, or by some person thereunto by him or her lawfully authorized, that is to say: (1) Every agreement that by its terms is not to be performed in one year from the making thereof; (2) every special promise to answer for the debt, default, or misdoings of another person; (3) every agreement, promise, or undertaking made upon consideration of marriage, except mutual promises to marry; (4) every special promise made by an executor or administrator to answer damages out of his or her own estate; (5) an agreement authorizing or employing an agent or broker to sell or purchase real estate for compensation or a commission. [ 2011 c 336 s 540 ; 1905 c 58 s 1 ; RRS s 5825. Prior: Code 1881 s 2325; 1863 p 412 s 2 ; 1860 p 298 s 2 ; 1854 p 403 s 2 .]

perturbation.justification: The modifications introduce ambiguity and weaken the indemnification obligations, creating a contradiction. The original contract language provides a clear obligation to defend and indemnify. The changed text, however, makes these obligations contingent and subject to interpretation. By adding "when feasible" to FNW's obligation and changing Sponsor's obligation to "use good faith efforts," the contract undermines the specificity required for enforceable contracts, as highlighted in the Law Explanation. RCW 19.36.010, as evidenced in both scraped snippets, requires agreements to be in writing; the introduced ambiguity weakens the enforceability of the written agreement, as the obligations are no longer clearly defined.
